prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: In the fateful year of our Lord two thousand and twenty-three, during the third quarter thereof, the noble court of CloudCorp, a venerable bastion of Software-as-a-Service, did gather to proclaim the sacred scrolls of their numerical sorcery. By the grace of algorithms, the mystical creatures known as "Users" hath increased by a count of seven hundred and sixty-five, a number wondrous and most peculiar, for it surpasseth the sum of our lordly liegemen by thrice. The scribes of finance did chant incantations of "ARR," invoking the arcane spirits of recurring revenue, which didst swell to the sum of one million, four hundred thousand golden florins, a treasure trove indeed. And so, in the hallowed halls of the Stock Market Keep, the banner of "Growth" was unfurled, and the minstrels sang of a land where ethereal clouds raineth profits upon the worthy and the wise.
